content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission
September 9,
1992
Don E.
Murphy
Chairman
Fair Oaks Citizens
Against New Taxes
5331 Ridgevale Way
Fair Oaks,
CA
95628
Re:
Your Request for Advice
Our File No.
A-92-455
Dear Mr.
Murphy:
This is in response to your request for confirmation of
telephone advice provided to you concerning the use of campaign
funds under the provisions of the Political Reform Act (the
“Act”) •1
This letter confirms the advice I provided to you on
August 18,
1992.
As we discussed, the Act imposes restrictions on
the use of campaign funds that are held by candidates and
committees.
(Section 89511; Section 89512.)
The general rule
2 is that an expenditure of campaign funds
must be reasonably related to a political, legislative,
or
governmental purpose.
However,
where an expenditure of campaign
funds confers a substantial personal benefit on a controlling
member of a committee, the expenditure must be directly related to
1
Government Code Sections 81000-91015.
All statutory references
are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.
Commission
regulations appear at
2 California Code of Regulations Section
18000,
All references to regulations are to Title 2,
Division
6 of the California Code of Regulations.
The Act also contains provisions which set out special
standards for specific expenditures.
(Sections 89513,
g.)
428 J Street, Suite 800 •
P.O. Box 807 •
Sacramento, CA
95812 •
(916) 322-5660


Our File No. A-92-455
Page
2
a political, legislative, or governmental purpose.
3
As we discussed,
each of the following expenditures appears
to be reasonably related to a political purpose:
1.
Initiating and supporting the recall effort against two
directors of the Fair Oaks Recreation and Park Board.
2.
supporting candidates for election and reelection to the
Fair Oaks Recreation and Park Board.
Thus,
you may use the committee’s campaign funds for these
expenditures.
4
You also asked about using campaign funds for attorney’s fees
and associated litigation costs incurred in challenging an action
of the Fair Oaks Recreation and Par1 Board pertaining to an
assessment.
Section 89514 provides:
Expenditures of campaign funds for attorney’s
fees and other costs in connection with
administrative, civil, or criminal litigation are
not directly related to a political, legislative,
or governmental purpose except where the litigation
is directly related to activities of a committee
that are consistent with its ,rimarv objectives or
arises directly out of a committee’s activities or
out of a candidate’s or elected officer’s
activities, duties, or status as a candidate or
elected officer, including, but not limited to,
an
action to enjoin defamation, defense of an action
to enjoin defamation, defense of an action brought
for a violation of state or local campaign,
disclosure, or election laws,
and an action arising
from an election contest or recount.
Emphasis added.
Thus,
so long as the attorney’s fees are incurred in
connection actions directly related to activities of the committee
“Substantial personal benefit” means an expenditure of campaign
funds which results in a direct personal benefit with a value of
more than $100 to a candidate, elected officer,
or any individual
or individuals with authority to approve the expenditure of
campaign funds held by a committee.
(Section 89511(b) (3) .).
You should contact the Commission’s Technical Assistance and
Analysis Divisions at
(916)
322-5662 for information on specific
reporting requirements.


Our File No. A-92-455
Page
3
which are consistent with the committee’s primary objectives,
campaign funds may be used to make the expenditures.
If you have any further questions regarding this matter,
please feel free to contact me at
(916)
322_5901.6
Sincerely,
Scott Hallabrin
Acting General Counsel
y:
John W. Wallace
ounsel,
Legal Division
SH:JWW:aa
Of course this advice is limited to the facts provided.
Other
types of litigation, not expressly set forth in Section 89514,
may
not be considered political in nature.
You should contact us for
further advice should other issues arise.
Copies of Commission regulations and Opinions are available in
many law libraries.
Alternatively, copies of thesematerials and
Commission advice letters may be obtained from the Commission at a
cost of 1Oc per page.

sections.question: May campaign funds be used for: (1) initiating and supporting a recall effort against two directors of the Fair Oaks Recreation and Park Board; (2) supporting candidates for election and reelection to the Fair Oaks Recreation and Park Board; and (3) attorney's fees and associated litigation costs incurred in challenging an action of the Fair Oaks Recreation and Park Board pertaining to an assessment?
sections.conclusion: The general rule is that an expenditure of campaign funds must be reasonably related to a political, legislative, or governmental purpose. The committee may use campaign funds for initiating and supporting the recall effort against the two directors and for supporting candidates for election and reelection to the Fair Oaks Recreation and Park Board, as these expenditures are reasonably related to a political purpose. For attorney's fees and litigation costs, such expenditures may be made if they are incurred in connection with actions directly related to activities of the committee that are consistent with its primary objectives, as set forth in Section 89514.

reporting requirements.

which are consistent with the committee’s primary objectives,
campaign funds may be used to make the expenditures.
sections.question_synthetic: The requestor asks whether campaign funds held by a committee may be used for recall efforts, candidate support, and litigation costs related to challenging a Park Board action regarding an assessment.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: Campaign funds may be used for the recall effort and candidate support as they are reasonably related to political purposes. Attorney's fees and litigation costs may be used only if they are directly related to the committee's activities consistent with its primary objectives, as specified in Section 89514.
sections.parsing_notes: Format: ocr; LLM: Document is a formal FPPC response letter confirming prior telephone advice. Contains clear question and conclusion sections with statutory references. OCR quality is good with minor formatting variations. File number A-92-455 corresponds to document ID.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: May campaign funds be used for: (1) initiating and supporting a recall effort against two directors of the Fair Oaks Recreation and Park Board; (2) supporting candidates for election and reelection to the Fair Oaks Recreation and Park Board; and (3) attorney's fees and associated litigation costs incurred in challenging an action of the Fair Oaks Recreation and Park Board pertaining to an assessment?

CONCLUSION: The general rule is that an expenditure of campaign funds must be reasonably related to a political, legislative, or governmental purpose. The committee may use campaign funds for initiating and supporting the recall effort against the two directors and for supporting candidates for election and reelection to the Fair Oaks Recreation and Park Board, as these expenditures are reasonably related to a political purpose. For attorney's fees and litigation costs, such expenditures may be made if they are incurred in connection with actions directly related to activities of the committee that are consistent with its primary objectives, as set forth in Section 89514.

embedding.summary: This FPPC advice letter confirms telephone advice regarding permissible uses of campaign funds under the Political Reform Act, specifically addressing recall efforts, candidate support, and litigation costs for a Fair Oaks citizens committee. The FPPC advises that certain expenditures are permissible while cautioning that litigation costs must meet specific statutory requirements.
